steps.title: Identify the text's central claim
steps.content: The passage introduces the silversword alliance, notes the plants' extraordinary range of phenotypes, then explains the cause: all descended from one tarweed ancestor and diversified as they adapted to varied Hawaiian habitats. The text is doing two things — describing the group and explaining its diversity.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To describe the silversword alliance and explain how the plant family became so varied': Matches both parts of the text exactly — the description of the alliance and the explanation of its diversity through adaptation. ✓
  • Choice B — 'To describe the specific habitat where Dubautia menziesii are found and identify other plants that share a common ancestor with them': Violates scope — the text never describes a specific habitat for Dubautia menziesii or identifies other plants sharing its ancestor. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To indicate the specific tarweed ancestor of all plants that grow on the Hawaiian Islands...': Violates scope — the tarweed is only the ancestor of the silversword alliance, not all Hawaiian plants, and the text does not explain why plants have varied characteristics beyond adaptation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'To advance the claim that all plants on the Hawaiian Islands are part of the silversword alliance...': Violates scope — the text explicitly says the silversword alliance grows only on Hawaii but never claims all Hawaiian plants belong to it; no list of ancestors is provided. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
